fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Vermont Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Vermont - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity vermont, how to transfer occupational therapist license to vermont, occupational therapist compact vermont, vermont occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Vermont Office of Professional Regulation — OT
board.mailing_address: Office of Professional Regulation, 89 Main Street, 3rd Floor, Montpelier, VT 05620-3402

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT)

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Vermont.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create an Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Register or log in to the Vermont OPR Online Services Portal and complete the Initial Application for Occupational Therapist Licensure. Pay the non-refundable application fee of $100.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your official transcripts, verifying completion of an accredited Occupational Therapy program, to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Vermont Office of Professional Regulation.

steps.title: Verify NBCOT Certification
steps.description: Ensure your certification with the National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) is current and request NBCOT to electronically verify your certification directly to the Vermont Office of Professional Regulation.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: All applicants must complete a criminal history records check. This involves scheduling an appointment for fingerprinting after submitting your online application. Instructions for scheduling and required forms are available on the Vermont OPR website.

steps.title: Provide Disciplinary History (if applicable)
steps.description: If you have ever been licensed in another state or jurisdiction, you must request a disciplinary history report from all relevant state licensing boards to be sent directly to the Vermont Office of Professional Regulation.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBCOT exam. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Vermont OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around  to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Vermont have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Vermont has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Vermont?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Vermont while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

hero.title: Become a Occupational Therapist in Vermont
hero.dek: To apply for an Occupational Therapist license in Vermont, you must submit your application and all supporting documents through the Vermont OPR Online Services Portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Go to the Vermont OPR Online Services Portal, click 'Register Now' or 'Create Account', and follow the prompts to set up your user ID and password. You will need to provide personal details and verify your email.
